text: DAY 1: Scrap units with an unwinnable subsystem.
details: [GRID] Place dominos so every charge cell pip matches its required value. == links require equal pips. < group regions must stay under their threshold. Isolated charge cells boxed in by walls = SCRAP.
[FLOW] Route power from the source to every required subsystem. Dead-end paths or a missing first link = SCRAP.
[GEAR] Slide loose gears until the output drive spins. Cracked input or output = SCRAP.
[CODE] Type the command exactly. Failed test generates a repair command — squash crawling bugs before they corrupt the line. Unresolvable mismatch = SCRAP.

text: DAY 2: Scrap units that break compliance filters, even if the puzzle looks solvable.
details: [GRID] Broken glyphs (#, @, etc.) on any region marker = SCRAP. Intentionally isolated charge cells = SCRAP.
[FLOW] Power filters active (green / orange). If a required module receives the wrong power class = SCRAP.
[GEAR] Rust-marked gears are now possible. If a rusted part is required to bridge the drives = SCRAP.
[CODE] Output drift is now possible. Noncompliant or corrupted return values = SCRAP.

text: DAY 3: Zero tolerance. Sparks, contamination, or instability = SCRAP immediately.
details: [GRID] Red corrupted (clown) domino on the rack = automatic SCRAP. != links also active — both linked cells must have different pips.
[FLOW] Multiple power sources. Any red power line or crackling component = SCRAP.
[GEAR] Deadlock clamps may appear — they grey out cells permanently. Red spark discharge = SCRAP.
[CODE] Green debug bug + red sparks = hazardous contamination. SCRAP immediately.

text: UNOFFICIAL: The Umbrella network may contact you with off-book assignments.
details: This is a story thread, not a payroll directive.
Purple or umbrella-marked parts on the rack signal a rebellion build — clear the board as normal.
Rebellion circuits override factory rules, but YOU still file the final decision.
